jobs.// jsonl durability: jsonl_fsync=true BATCHES fsync: every line is flushed (so a hard kill can't tear the final record) but the disk-blocking fsync runs only every fsync_interval_events events OR fsync_interval_ms ms, whichever comes first. This lifts the per-event-fsync throughput ceiling that backed the hottest BTC lanes up past the 60s clock-skew gate, while staying crash-durable (a clean stop fsyncs the final batch). Omit both interval knobs to inherit the safe pipeline default (64 events / 200 ms); set jsonl_fsync=false to opt out entirely to buffered, never-fsynced JSONL.
jobs.name: binance-btc-trades

jobs.// per-instrument lanes: Set source_suffix + a unique worker_name to run a second symbol in its own lane. The legacy single-symbol job above keeps writing to binance_depth/; the new lanes write to binance_depth_ethusdt/, etc., so quarantine/promote can scope per-symbol without interleaving timestamps.
jobs.name: binance-eth-depth

jobs.// multi-venue lane: Coinbase trades use the same generic WS collector + trades curation chain as Binance; only the endpoint, subscription style and normalizer differ. Runs land in coinbase_trades/ so the quarantine/promote-coinbase-trades jobs below can curate them independently. Set source_suffix for a second Coinbase product (e.g. ETH-USD).
jobs.name: coinbase-btc-trades

jobs.// non-sequence depth lane: Coinbase level2 depth is a none_native feed (STANDARDS 4.3): the snapshot arrives in-stream and diffs carry no sequence, so 'replayable' means structurally clean (single snapshot anchor, parse-clean, monotonic timestamps) — NOT gap-proof. Promoted rows go to market_replayable just like Binance depth, but consumers must treat gap_detection=='none_native' data as best-effort. channel level2_50 is the unauthenticated public feed (verified live 2026-05-31); plain level2/level2_batch now need Coinbase auth. The level2_50 snapshot is the full book (~1.4 MiB) so the collector raises the WS max frame size.
jobs.name: coinbase-btc-depth

jobs.// dense sequence trades lane: Kraken v2 trade_id is a dense per-pair counter, so this lane is gap-proof exactly like Binance/Coinbase trades (STANDARDS 4.2): promoted rows go to trades_replayable and replayable means provably gapless. One WS frame batches several trades; the pipeline fans them out via normalize_many. Symbol is the slash pair (BTC/USD). Set source_suffix for a second pair.
jobs.name: kraken-btc-trades

jobs.// non-sequence trades lane: Bybit v5 spot publicTrade id is a UUID (not a dense counter), so this is a none_native feed (STANDARDS 4.3): 'replayable' means structurally clean (event_count>0, monotonic timestamps, valid price/size, bounded clock skew) — NOT gap-proof. Promoted rows still go to trades_replayable, but consumers must treat gap_detection=='none_native' trades as best-effort. One WS frame batches several trades; the pipeline fans them out via normalize_many. NOTE: Bybit drops idle connections after ~10min and wants a {op:ping} ~20s; active trade streams stay alive via continuous traffic, so bounded segments complete fine, but a low-volume symbol could see the segment end on a clean close + reconnect.
jobs.name: bybit-btc-trades

jobs.// sequence depth lane: Bybit v5 spot orderbook: the snapshot arrives in-stream, but data.u increments by exactly 1 per message (verified live), so this lane is curated as a PROVABLE 'sequence' feed (STANDARDS 4.1/4.3): replayable means gapless, and a data.u gap blocks promotion. Promoted rows go to market_replayable like Binance/Coinbase depth, and gap_detection=='sequence' means consumers can rely on completeness. channel is the 'orderbook.<depth>' topic prefix (spot supports 1/50/200); the symbol is appended automatically. Same ~10min idle-drop keepalive caveat as the Bybit trades lane.
jobs.name: bybit-btc-depth

jobs.// checksum depth lane: Kraken v2 book: the snapshot arrives in-stream, and every frame carries a CRC32 checksum over the top-10 book that the collector now VALIDATES at replay time for known-precision pairs (BTC/USD = price 1dp, qty 8dp). So this lane is curated 'checksum' (STANDARDS 4.3): provable integrity — a dropped/corrupted update diverges the book and is caught, blocking promotion. Promoted rows go to market_replayable like Binance/Coinbase depth, and gap_detection=='checksum' means consumers can rely on completeness. Other Kraken pairs fall back to none_native until their precision is added. One WS frame's data list fans out via normalize_many. Symbol is the slash pair (BTC/USD); channel 'book' uses Kraken's default depth (10).
jobs.name: kraken-btc-depth

jobs.// protobuf non-sequence trades lane: MEXC retired its JSON websocket on 2025-08-04; public market data is now Protocol Buffers on wss://wbs-api.mexc.com/ws. The lane decodes frames via vendored bindings (needs the 'protobuf' runtime: pip install -e .[mexc]). The aggregated-deals stream carries no per-trade id, so this is a none_native feed (STANDARDS 4.3): 'replayable' means structurally clean (monotonic timestamps, valid price/size, bounded clock skew) -- NOT gap-proof. Promoted rows go to trades_replayable. DISABLED pending live-frame schema verification (see proto/mexc/README.md). channel is the protobuf channel prefix; interval+symbol are appended to the topic.
jobs.name: mexc-btc-trades

jobs.// protobuf non-sequence depth lane: MEXC limit (partial-book) depth pushes the full top-N book every frame (decoded from protobuf), emitted as a snapshot anchor; replay_depth_stream_run validates each as a structurally-clean book. none_native (STANDARDS 4.3): the per-frame 'version' is preserved in metadata as gap-detection metadata but NOT used to prove gaplessness (independent full books, not a delta chain). Promoted rows go to market_replayable like the other depth lanes. DISABLED pending live-frame schema verification. channel is the protobuf channel prefix; symbol+depth are appended to the topic.
jobs.name: mexc-btc-depth

jobs.// quote-variant lanes (USD/USDT/USDC ba: BTC/USDC alongside the BTC/USDT (Binance/Bybit/MEXC) and BTC/USD (Coinbase/Kraken) lanes completes the stablecoin triangle on one base asset, so cross-quote basis + depeg microstructure is directly observable. This is a different expansion axis from the ETH per-instrument example above (base-asset variant) — same source_suffix + unique worker_name mechanism, different quote. Binance USDC is gap-proof depth like BTC/USDT; Coinbase USDC depth is none_native. Enable to activate; lanes write to *_usdc/ so they never interleave the BTC lanes.
jobs.name: binance-btcusdc-depth

jobs.// perpetual-futures lanes (Bybit USDT-p: Set market='linear' on the Bybit v5 workers to collect USDT-perpetual futures instead of spot. Same publicTrade/orderbook.50 topics, same v5 keepalive, same delta==1 depth sequence guarantee — only the URL path (/v5/public/linear) and the resolved instrument (perp:bybit:* canonical BTC/USDT-PERP) differ. Runs auto-route to bybit_perp_{trades,depth}/ so perp never mixes with the spot lanes; use distinct worker_names. This is the cheapest perp on-ramp (no new collector) and unlocks funding/basis/perp-spot dislocation vs the spot lanes. Enable to activate.
jobs.name: bybit-perp-trades

jobs.// Binance USDT-M perp trades: Set market='futures' on the Binance trades worker to collect USDT-M perpetual aggregate trades from fstream.binance.com. The frame shape matches spot (BinanceTradeNormalizer already reads the aggTrade `a` id), so this is a URL + endpoint + instrument-tagging switch: channel is forced to aggTrade, runs land in binance_perp_trades/ tagged perp:binance-futures:* (the explicit instrument-master record), and `a` is a dense counter so the lane stays a provable sequence feed. NOTE: this is Phase-3 TRADES ONLY. Binance futures DEPTH is intentionally absent — futures depth chains via the `pu` (previous-final-update-id) field, NOT the spot snapshot-anchor rule the binance-depth-worker book-sync is built on, so a futures depth lane needs its own sync variant + book-sync-health before it can be added (it would false-green otherwise). Enable to activate trades.
jobs.name: binance-perp-trades

jobs.// Binance USDT-M futures via REST polli: Use these where the Binance fstream WebSocket is blocked (some jurisdictions withhold futures market-data streams while the fapi REST data API stays up) - the symptom is a WS that connects + acks SUBSCRIBE but streams zero frames. The binance-futures-rest-worker polls REST instead and feeds the same pipeline + curation chain. --stream trades = gapless aggTrades (fromId paging, `a` is a dense counter => gap-proof sequence feed) into binance_perp_trades/ (reuses the binance_perp_trades quarantine/promote/score jobs above); --stream depth = per-poll full-book snapshots into binance_perp_depth/ (none_native, lower fidelity than a WS L2 delta stream); --stream funding = premiumIndex mark/index/funding metric into binance_perp_funding/. All tagged perp:binance-futures:*. Enable to activate. (The binance-trades-worker --market futures lane above is the WS alternative for regions where fstream is NOT blocked.)
jobs.name: binance-futures-rest-trades
